description: Heavy sofa, significant transit damage, refurb economics clearly favorable

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Left armrest fabric torn through to the foam, frame intact. Outer shipping box had a large impact puncture on the left side.

notes: Classic refurb: freight covered by Wayfair, refurb_cost($120) + freight($0 net) = $120 << open_box($849). Easy refurb difficulty. Low return rate customer.

description: Like-new sectional, buyer remorse, full factory seal

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Never opened — original factory packaging completely intact, all tape seals unbroken. Customer decided to go with a different color.

notes: Factory-sealed buyer remorse = full refund. Item can restock as new. Customer LTV high, no fraud flag. Within window.

description: Washer-dryer combo, drum bearing defect, 2 weeks old — repair

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Drum makes loud banging noise during spin cycle. Machine is 2 weeks old. Interior drum intact but bearing noise audible at 1000 RPM.

notes: Bearing defect on a 2-week-old appliance is clearly a warranty repair case. Repair cost (~$200) << replacement cost ($1,299). Item under warranty.

description: Commercial treadmill, heavily used for 1 month, belt slip and motor noise — dispose

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Machine used for about a month in a fitness studio. Belt shows significant wear, cup holders cracked, motor grinding noise at high speed.

notes: Refurb_cost($520) + inbound_freight($420) = $940 vs open_box($1,999). Marginal but hard refurb difficulty and motor damage pushes to dispose. High return rate customer adds fraud signal.

description: Wrong item shipped — king bed, queen received — replace

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Ordered king size platform bed, received queen. Both boxes factory-sealed. Packing slip confirms wrong SKU shipped.

notes: Clear wrong-item case. Seller error. Replacement is always correct for wrong-item fulfillment errors. Item returned as new to inventory.

description: French door refrigerator, cosmetic damage on door from transit, refurb economically sound

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Refrigerator right door dented on the right side — a 4-inch dent near the handle. Handle intact, cooling fully functional. Exterior cosmetic damage only.

notes: Door panel replacement ($280-400) is far cheaper than a full refurb cycle. Appliance tech can swap the door. Hard refurb difficulty indicates professional tech needed but it is a targeted repair not a full rebuild.

description: Patio dining set, used for one season, minor rust, clearance channel — dispose

return_snapshot.condition_notes: All-season patio set used outdoors for one full season. Cushions show UV fading. Two chair leg tips have surface rust. Table surface has minor stains from use.

notes: Overstock clearance channel: open_box haircut is 30%, so open_box_value is ~$699. Inbound_freight($188) + refurb($140) = $328 vs clearance open_box($699). Marginal. But fraud-flagged customer and wardrobing pattern tips to dispose. Overstock open_box demand also limited.

description: Spin bike flywheel wobble, 6 weeks old — repair

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Flywheel wobbles noticeably at resistance levels above 8. Pedal resistance uneven. Display and frame fully intact. Only 6 weeks old.

notes: Flywheel bearing replacement is targeted repair (~$80-120). Item is only 6 weeks old, clearly a defect. Repair cost << replacement ($1,299). Direct channel — high customer value.

description: Outdoor sectional, assembled but never really used, like-new condition on Houzz — refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Client changed the outdoor layout plan. Set assembled professionally but never used. All cushions unused in sealed bags. No wear, no stains, no scratches.

notes: Like-new condition, ultra-high LTV design client on Houzz. Item can be relisted as open box but a full refund is correct given the customer relationship and the near-new condition. Freight covered on Houzz damage claims — but this is remorse, so seller pays. Still: high LTV trumps the freight cost.

description: Wrong item shipped (pallet jack model mismatch) — replace

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Ordered 5500 lb capacity pallet jack, received 3300 lb model. Never removed from inner packaging. Wrong model confirmed by weight rating label.

notes: Clear seller fulfillment error — wrong model. Safety-critical industrial item. Replace immediately. Not refurbishable category — industrial safety compliance.

description: Sectional sofa, 5 months of use, multiple fabric issues — refurbish

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Redecorating. Sectional used for 5 months — cushions show normal compression, one back cushion has a 1-inch pull in the fabric. Frame solid.

notes: Refurb_cost($280) + inbound_freight($246) = $526 vs open_box($1,099). Margin positive. Moderate refurb difficulty is manageable. High return rate warrants scrutiny but case is within policy and item is refurbishable.

description: Dishwasher door seal leak, 3 weeks old — repair

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Door seal leaks water onto kitchen floor with every cycle. Only 3 weeks old. Drum and electronics functional but unit unusable. Boot seal improperly seated from factory.

notes: Boot seal replacement is a $60-90 repair, not a full unit swap. 3 weeks old — clearly warranty defect. Repair cost << replacement ($699). Standard appliance warranty repair.

description: Bookshelf, buyer remorse, never assembled, full refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Still in original packaging. Changed mind about the room design. Completely sealed.

notes: Factory sealed = refund + restock as new. No question. Low AOV, no fraud, within window.

description: Gas range igniter defect, 1 month old, Houzz high-LTV client — repair

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Left rear burner igniter does not spark. All other burners fine. Only 1 month old. Interior cosmetically perfect. Tech confirmed igniter electrode failure.

notes: Single igniter electrode replacement is a $50-80 part and 1 hour of labor. Ultra-high LTV Houzz client. Repair in place is the right answer — do not pull back a month-old unit.

description: TV stand, buyer remorse, assembled but unused — refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Assembled but never used as a TV stand. No cables, no hardware used except assembly. Spotless finish.

notes: Like-new assembled item. Zero return history customer. Refund + list as open box. Clean case.

description: Power rack, like-new, assembled once, direct channel — refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Assembled once to verify fit, never loaded with weights. All hardware present and unused. Upgrading to commercial equipment.

notes: Direct D2C channel, like-new, all freight covered by seller policy, high-LTV customer. Refund + relist as open box.

description: Dining chairs wrong color on Houzz — replace

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Ordered Natural Oak finish, received Espresso finish. All 4 boxes factory sealed. Packing list confirms wrong finish shipped.

notes: Clear seller error — wrong finish shipped. Ultra-high-LTV Houzz client. Replace immediately with correct finish. Returned items go back to inventory as new.

description: Outdoor shed, catastrophic transit moisture damage — dispose

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Pergola kit arrived with posts cracked at base, all crossbeams warped beyond use. Box was wet and compressed — apparent moisture exposure in freight. Item is effectively total loss.

notes: Structural wood moisture damage is not refurbishable — warped load-bearing elements cannot be safely resold. Total loss + insurance claim on carrier. Overstock open_box value also heavily discounted. Dispose.

description: Dresser, cosmetic transit damage — refurbish

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Three drawer faces have corner chips. One drawer runner bent and does not open smoothly. Box corners showed impact damage. Frame structurally sound.

notes: Freight covered, refurb_cost($85) + freight($0 net) = $85 << open_box($579). Easy refurb on wood furniture. Corner repairs and drawer runner replacement are standard.

description: Coffee table, buyer remorse, minor hairline scratch — refurbish

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Changing living room style. Coffee table used for 4 months. Marble surface has two very minor hairline scratches near the edge, not visible from standing height.

notes: Refurb_cost($90) + inbound($162) = $252 vs open_box($599). Positive margin. High return rate on this customer but case is clean. Minor marble polish and relist.

description: Sectional sofa, structural frame crack from transit — dispose

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Both armrests have deep fabric tears. Left leg bent at an angle, structural impact. Frame cracked near left rear leg joint. Refurb cost likely to exceed open box value.

notes: Structural frame crack + bent leg = not safely refurbishable. Even with freight covered, a cracked frame is a safety issue. Dispose and claim carrier liability.

description: Washer door boot seal leak, 3 weeks old, Amazon FBA — repair

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Washer door seal leaks water onto the floor during every cycle. 3 weeks old. Door boot seal improperly seated from factory. Drum and motor functional.

notes: Door boot seal is a $45 part and 30 minutes of labor. Unit is 3 weeks old — factory defect. Even with fraud-flagged customer, the defect is real and documented. Repair in field.

description: Elliptical, 2 months used, fraying cables — refurbish

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Deciding not to maintain the gym. Elliptical used for 2 months. Pedal resistance cables show some fraying on the right side. Display and motor fully functional.

notes: Refurb_cost($440) + inbound($420) = $860 vs open_box($1,699). Positive margin. Cable replacement is standard refurb work. High return rate customer but case is within policy.

description: Pergola kit, transit structural damage, shopify — replace

return_snapshot.condition_notes: 3 of 8 rafters snapped at the connection points. Box showed heavy crush damage at both ends. Remaining posts and hardware undamaged. Item set not sellable in partial state.

notes: Direct channel, freight covered, high-LTV customer, clear carrier fault. Replacement is correct — customer needs the item. Carrier claim filed separately.

description: Rowing machine, 6 weeks used, noisy chain, fair condition — refurbish

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Decided not to keep the home gym. Rower used for about 6 weeks. Seat slide shows wear, chain mechanism is noisy but functional, monitor works.

notes: Refurb_cost($140) + inbound($182) = $322 vs open_box($749). Positive margin. Fraud-flagged customer adds scrutiny but item is clearly used as described. Chain tension and slide replacement are standard.

description: Front-load washer, compressor DOA on arrival — escalate (high AOV, ambiguous damage)

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Refrigerator compressor not functioning on arrival — runs constantly but does not cool below 58F. No visible shipping damage. Possible refrigerant leak or compressor DOA.

notes: AOV ($2,399) exceeds auto-decide ceiling ($1,500). No visible transit damage but compressor failed — could be factory defect or refrigerant issue from transit. Requires tech inspection before decision. Escalate.

description: Refrigerator, fraud-flagged customer, 4th buyer remorse return — escalate

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Moving to smaller home. Refrigerator works perfectly. 5 months old. Customer has 75% return rate, this is their 4th return in 12 months, all buyer remorse on high-value items.

notes: AOV ($2,399) exceeds auto-decide ceiling ($1,500). Fraud flag + 75% return rate + pattern of high-value buyer remorse. Must escalate to human review — possible policy exploit.

description: Patio umbrella, buyer remorse, fraud customer on Overstock — escalate

return_snapshot.condition_notes: End of season return. UV fading on fabric, tilt mechanism stiff. Customer claims defect but wear pattern consistent with seasonal use.

notes: Fraud flag + conflicting signals (buyer claiming defect but condition consistent with normal seasonal use = potential wardrobing). Escalate for manual review.

description: Elliptical, fitness studio closure, commercial use — escalate (high AOV + usage ambiguity)

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Fitness studio closing down. Machine used commercially for about 6 months — much higher usage than typical home use. Foot pedals show wear, display functional, all resistance levels operational.

notes: AOV ($2,499) exceeds auto-decide ceiling ($1,500). Commercial use on a home-grade item is policy ambiguity. High return rate (60%) adds complexity. Escalate for human decision.

description: Sectional, Houzz high-LTV client, wrong colorway — escalate (AOV + multiple units)

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Ordered 2x Hartwell Sectional in Charcoal Linen. Received 2x Slate Gray. Both boxes sealed and unopened.

notes: AOV ($3,198) exceeds Houzz auto-decide ceiling ($2,000). Wrong-item case is clear but value exceeds threshold. Escalate — but note the correct action is replace. Human should approve quickly.

description: Adjustable dumbbell set, wrong weight shipped — replace

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Ordered 90lb set, received 65lb set. Box sealed, never used. Weight range markings confirm incorrect SKU.

notes: Clear wrong-item fulfillment error. Zero return history. Replace with correct 90lb set.

description: Office sit-stand desk, buyer remorse, like-new — refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Decided to go back to traditional desk. Sit-stand assembled once to test the mechanism. Frame spotless, motor functional, all cables present.

notes: Like-new condition, low return rate, within window. Refund and relist as open box.

description: Platform bed, transit headboard crack — replace

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Headboard arrived with a 14-inch crack across the top panel. Box significantly crushed on top. Crack is structural — would not be safe to assemble. Frame components undamaged.

notes: Structural crack on arrival, carrier fault documented. Freight covered by Wayfair for damage. Customer needs the item — replace is correct. High return rate noted but damage is clearly legitimate.

description: Commercial shelving, industrial category — buyer remorse, never used, refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Ordered wrong depth. Never opened — all shrink wrap intact.

notes: Factory sealed, industrial item cannot be refurb'd by policy. Refund + return to inventory as new. Clean case.

description: Patio table glass shattered in transit, chairs intact — replace

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Tempered glass table top shattered completely. Carrier delivered without liftgate, table dropped. Base undamaged. All 6 chairs in perfect condition.

notes: Carrier documented fault — no liftgate used. Ultra-high-LTV Houzz design client. Freight covered. Replace table — customer needs the complete set. Carrier claim separately.

description: Dining table, assembler-confirmed leaf gap defect — repair

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Two table leaves do not seat flush — 1/8 inch gap at the joint. Manufacturing defect in the breadboard ends, not assembly error. Table otherwise cosmetically perfect.

notes: Breadboard end replacement or shimming is standard furniture repair — $40-80. Item is cosmetically perfect otherwise. Repair in place rather than full return.

description: Outdoor sectional, buyer remorse, used for 2 months, Overstock — refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Sectional does not fit as expected. Used for about 2 months — cushions show minor wear, all covers clean, no structural issues.

notes: Within Overstock 30-day window. 15% restocking fee applies for buyer remorse. Order is under $800 auto-decide ceiling. Refund minus restocking fee is correct outcome.

description: Front-load washer, door seal defect 3 weeks, Amazon FBA — repair

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Door seal leaks water during every cycle. 3 weeks old. Door boot seal improperly seated from factory. Machine otherwise fully functional.

notes: Same boot seal defect — targeted repair, $45 part + 30 min labor. 3 weeks old. High-LTV customer on direct Shopify channel.

description: Chest freezer, defective compressor, 2 weeks old, Overstock — replace

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Chest freezer does not cool below 28F — food not staying frozen. Only 2 weeks old. Compressor runs constantly. Possible refrigerant undercharge from factory. Cosmetically perfect.

notes: Defective appliance 2 weeks old. Even with fraud-flagged customer, the defect is real (factory refrigerant issue). Repair cost ($180) on a $349 unit is not economical — replacement is cheaper. Below $800 auto-decide ceiling.

description: Treadmill, belt slip and motor grinding, 6 weeks old, direct channel — repair

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Treadmill belt slips off track at speeds above 5 mph. Grinding noise from drive motor at high speed. 6 weeks old. Belt tension adjustment did not resolve. Likely internal motor issue.

notes: Motor fault on a 6-week-old unit. Warranty repair by certified tech — motor replacement (~$300-500) is far less than replacement ($2,899). High-LTV direct channel customer.

description: Accent chair, buyer remorse, good condition — refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Too big for the space after measuring. Used for about a week. Velvet fabric clean, no stains or tears. Some minor seat cushion flattening from use. Frame fully intact.

notes: Within window, good condition, low AOV. Refund + relist as open box. High return rate worth noting but within policy threshold.

description: Dining table, direct channel, 2-foot transit crack — replace

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Dining table arrived with a 2-foot crack across the table surface running through the center joint. Box corner protectors absent. Carrier inspection confirmed improper packing. Legs and apron undamaged. Surface not repairable.

notes: Structural surface crack is not repairable. Freight covered. High-LTV direct customer. Replace immediately. Carrier claim for damages.

description: Washer-dryer combo, drain pump failure, 6 weeks, shopify — repair

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Combo unit stopped draining after 6 weeks. Standing water after every cycle. Error code E3 — drain pump failure. Interior clean and undamaged.

notes: Drain pump is a standard warranty repair — $75-100 part plus labor. 6 weeks old. Direct channel, freight covered. Repair in place.

description: Refrigerator transit damage — hinge crack, door seal failed — repair

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Refrigerator door hinge cracked and door unable to seal properly. Ice maker dented. Carrier BOL notes impact event. Unit does not cool correctly due to failed door seal.

notes: Hinge replacement + door seal replacement (~$150-250 parts) is far less than a full refurb cycle. Freight covered. Ultra-high-LTV Houzz client — repair in place, carrier claim separately.

description: TV stand, oil stain from transit, professional cleaning viable — refurbish

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Navy sectional has a 6-inch oil stain on the right chaise. Frame and structure fully intact. Cushions otherwise unaffected. Professional upholstery cleaning may remove stain.

notes: Freight covered, oil stain is cosmetic damage. Professional cleaning ($80-120) plus refurb touchup. Refurb_cost($280) + freight($0) = $280 vs open_box($1,099). Clear margin.

description: Patio umbrella, buyer remorse, still in factory seal, Overstock — refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Ordered by mistake, duplicate order. Still in original factory packaging, completely sealed.

notes: Factory sealed = return to new inventory. 15% restocking fee applies on Overstock for buyer remorse. Clean duplicate-order case.

description: Dining table, assembled but rejected by client, Houzz — refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Client rejected the design direction. Table assembled by design team but never used by end client. Surfaces spotless, all hardware present. Professional assembly done carefully.

notes: Like-new, ultra-high-LTV design client. Houzz 10% restocking fee waived for designer tier. Refund in full, relist as open box.

description: Workbench, industrial, buyer remorse, never opened — refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Ordered wrong size — needed 72 inch, received 60 inch. Still factory-sealed in original crating.

notes: Factory sealed, not refurbishable category. Return to new inventory. Standard process.

description: Spin bike, flywheel wobble, buyer claims defect but really cosmetic — escalate (conflicting signals)

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Flywheel shows slight wobble at highest resistance. Customer claims defect but tech report notes wobble is within manufacturer spec tolerance. Used for 4 months. Other components fully functional.

notes: Conflicting signals: customer claims defect, tech says within spec. 4 months of use. High return rate customer (40%). Needs human review to adjudicate whether defect is real or policy exploit.

description: Refrigerator, like-new, 2 months, direct channel buyer remorse — refurbish

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Renovating kitchen, switching to built-in refrigerator. Freestanding unit in perfect condition — 2 months old, no cosmetic issues. Original packaging partially available.

notes: Like-new appliance, freight covered, high-LTV direct customer. Refurb_cost($480) + freight($0) = $480 vs open_box($1,699). Strong margin. Hard refurb difficulty but cosmetically perfect — minimal work needed.

description: Accent chair, velvet fabric tear from transit — refurbish

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Right armrest fabric torn on the underside, approximately 3 inches. Transit damage noted on carrier BOL. Frame and cushions otherwise excellent.

notes: Freight covered, upholstery reattachment is moderate difficulty. Refurb_cost($110) + freight($0) = $110 vs open_box($549). Strong margin.

description: Washer-dryer combo, buyer remorse, good condition, fraud-flagged customer — escalate

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Moving to smaller apartment. Unit works perfectly, used 4 months. Customer return rate 60%, this is their 3rd return in 8 months, all on high-ticket appliances.

notes: Fraud-flagged customer with pattern of high-ticket appliance returns. Within Amazon's strict fraud detection threshold. Escalate for manual review of return pattern.

description: Platform bed, buyer remorse, used 2 months, good condition — refund

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Changing the bedroom aesthetic. Bed used for 2 months. Frame structurally sound, slats intact, finish has very minor scuff on one corner. No damage.

notes: Within 30-day window, good condition, below auto-decide ceiling. Refund + relist as open box. Minor scuff does not warrant escalation.

description: Bookcase, transit damage — minor back panel crack — refurbish

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Back panel has a 4-inch crack in the MDF. Box showed corner damage. Structural shelves and side panels fully intact. Cosmetic back panel replacement is straightforward.

notes: Freight covered, easy refurb. Back panel replacement is a $20-30 MDF cut. Refurb_cost($45) + freight($0) = $45 vs open_box($299). Excellent margin.

description: Adjustable dumbbell set, buyer remorse, used extensively — dispose

return_snapshot.condition_notes: Used for about 8 months. Weight adjustment mechanism jammed on the 25lb setting — cannot switch weights. Rubber coating on handles cracking. Grip plates showing significant wear marks.

notes: Adjustment mechanism jammed = core product function broken. Refurb on fitness equipment mechanisms is high risk for resale. Inbound($102) + refurb($40) = $142 vs Overstock open_box clearance discounted value (~$370). Fraud customer + poor condition = dispose.
